title: April 1-7
content:
Notable Incidents
Theft
A representative of a local retail business reported theft of a steak. Police reviewed CCTV footage and were able to identify a licence plate number of the subject vehicle involved.  This matter is still under investigation.
Counterfeit bills
Police are investigating after receiving a report that several counterfeit bills were passed at a local retail business.
Stolen Vehicle
Police assisted Edmonton Police Service with the matter of a suspicious vehicle (registered in Camrose)found in a ditch. Edmonton Police Service requested Camrose Police speak with the registered owner, which confirmed that the subject vehicle was stolen. The registered owner had parked the vehicle at the Edmonton Airport.
Breach conditions
Police conducted a traffic stop on suspicion of impaired driving. The 54-year-old male operator of the vehicle was a suspended driver. The male was also arrested for breach of conditions and lodged in Camrose Police Service cells.
Speeding
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for speeding. The 42-year-old male operator of the vehicle was issued violation tickets as it was determined he did not have a valid operator’s licence.
Causing disturbance
A representative of a local motel reported a male refusing to leave the property. Police attended, located and arrested the 33-year-old male subject for public intoxication. The male was transported to Camrose Police Service cells and released when sober.
Stolen Phone
A person reported a cell phone stolen after being accidently left at a local convenience store. Police reviewed CCTV footage from the store noting a male suspect. This matter is still under investigation.
Fail to produce
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for a traffic violation. The 31-year-old female operator of the vehicle was issued violation tickets for failing to produce insurance and registration and driving without a licence. The passenger was issued a violation ticket for improper use of a seatbelt.
C
ausing issues
A person reported a male subject causing issues at a local gas station. Police attended, located and arrested the 42-year-old male for public intoxication. The male was transported to Camrose Police Service cells and released when sober.
Underage possession of alcohol
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for suspicion of impaired driving. The youth passenger was issued a violation ticket for possession of open liquor in a motor vehicle.
Outstanding warrants
Police arrested a 28-year-old male for outstanding warrants.
Break and Enter
On April 7 at 0426 hours police responded to a break and enter in progress at a business in the area of 56 Street and 48 Avenue. Police attended , cleared the premises and determined the suspect had left prior to police arrival. It was determined that prefilled disposable vapes were stolen from the business. Investigation is ongoing.
Theft
A representative of a local retail business reported a male and female suspect stole merchandise from the business. The person reporting provided police with a description of the suspects and subject vehicle. Police located the subject vehicle and the two suspects, a 25-year-old female and 30-year-old male. Police issued trespass notices and the stolen merchandise was recovered.
Impaired driver
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for a traffic violation. The 21-year-old male operator of the vehicle failed an Alcohol Screening test. The male was arrested for impaired driving and breaching conditions of a release order. The vehicle was towed.
Causing concerns
A person reported a male subject causing issues inside a residence. The 15-year-old male was subsequently arrested and transported to Camrose Police Service cells to prevent further breach of the peace.
Theft
A representative of a local construction business reported the theft of $3500 worth of tools from a trailer parked in the area of 52A Street and 4900 Block . The matter is still under investigation.
Assault
A person reported being assaulted by another male subject known to him. Police attended and arrested a 15-year-old male for assault, uttering threats and mischief.

title: April 8-14
content:
Police attended 2 collisions. No injuries reported.
Police assisted EMS with 3 medical emergencies.
Notable Incidents
Outstanding warrant
Police arrested a 63-year-old male on an outstanding warrant.
Stolen wallet
A person reported their vehicle had been broken into and their wallet was stolen and identity was used to obtain credit cards.  This matter is still under investigation.
Theft
A representative from a local retail store reported an attempted theft by a female suspect. They provided police with a description of the subject vehicle and suspect. Police later identified the 30-year-old female subject and issued trespass notices.
Fire
A person reported their garage on fire. Police, along with Camrose Fire Department, attended. The surrounding area was evacuated during the incident to prevent risk to the public. An investigation continues.
Assault
A person reported being assaulted with a taser by a male known to them.  Police attended and arrested a 50-year-old male and transported him to Camrose Police Service cells. The male was charged with assault with a weapon and possession of a prohibited weapon.
No insurance/registration
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for a traffic violation. The 78-year-old male operator of the vehicle had no insurance and registration for the vehicle. The vehicle was towed.
Illegal parking
A person reported several vehicles parked in a “no parking” zone in the area of 50 Street and 44 Avenue.  Police attended and issued parking violation tickets.
Stolen property
A person reported their dirt bike (no motor) had been stolen by, who they believed to be, a female and male subject known to them. This matter is still under investigation.
Stolen vehicle
Police conducted an investigation on suspicion of a stolen vehicle purchased by an unsuspecting person through an online market. The vehicle was seized and towed. Follow-up investigation determined the registered owner of the stolen vehicle, who was subsequently notified by police.
Disturbance
Staff of a local motel requested police assistance regarding eviction of tenants causing issues. Police attended and arrested one of the subject tenants, a 40-year-old male, for previous breaches of his 24-hour curfew and possession of a weapon contrary to his release order. The male was lodged in Camrose Police Service cells. The other tenants were told to vacate the motel.
Traffic violation
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for a traffic violation. The 21-year-old female operator of the vehicle was given an Alcohol Screening test resulting in a caution. The vehicle was towed.
Theft
Staff of a local retail business reported the theft of a $300 pair of boots by a male subject, (description provided to police.) Police attended and reviewed CCTV footage. Investigation continues.
Damage to vehicles
A person reported their truck window was damaged while parked in the area of 44 Street and the 4900 Block. Police investigated, noting five additional vehicles had been damaged in the area.
On a separate incident a person reported their truck window was smashed while parked at a local hotel.
Outstanding warrants
A person reported one male and two females near a local motel, alleging the male was carrying a knife. Police attended and located the three subjects. Police determined there was no threat to public safety concerning the male subject. A female was arrested for an outstanding warrant.
Causing issues
A person reported a male subject, known to them, causing issues at their residence (garage.) Police attended and located the intoxicated 33-year-old male who became threatening. Once police withdrew a taser the male subject became cooperative and was subsequently arrested without issue. The male was transported and lodged at Camrose Police Service cells, then released once sober.
Theft
Staff at a local retail store reported a male subject, who was still on site, had stole a $90 pair of goggles. Police attended and issued the 19-year-old male a trespass notice.
Outstanding warrant
A 40-year-old male subject attended Camrose Police Service and was arrested on an outstanding warrant.
Stolen bike
A person reported their bike stolen in the area of a downtown church. Police attended and reviewed the church CCTV footage revealing a male subject taking the bike in question. This matter is still under investigation.
Fraud
A person reported being defrauded out of $300 by purchasing gift cards and giving them to an unknown male.
Outstanding warrants
Police arrested a 52-year-old male on an outstanding warrant.
Warrants
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for equipment violations. The 38-year-old male operator of the vehicle was issued a ticket for driving while suspended from doing so and the vehicle was towed.

title: April 16-21
content:
Police attended two collisions, one reporting minor injuries.
Police assisted with three medical emergencies.
Notable Incidents
Theft from vehicle
A person reported someone had broken into a truck in the area of 52 Street and 5400 Block and items, including a backpack containing lifting equipment and a wallet were stolen. This matter is still under investigation.
Impaired driving
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for a traffic violation. The 23-year-old female operator was determined to be impaired and issued an Immediate Roadside Sanction and violations tickets. Police noted open liquor in the vehicle. The 14-year-old female passenger initially lied about her age and subsequently was arrested for obstruction and housed in Camrose Police Service cells, later released to her mother.
Warrants
Police observed a female known to police, sleeping in her vehicle behind a business. Police spoke with the 26-year-old female and a check revealed she was wanted for outstanding warrants. A check on the 33-year-old male passenger also revealed he had warrants. Both were transported to Camrose Police Service station to deal with the outstanding matters.
Erratic driving
A person reported their vehicle damaged (minor not reportable) by a male subject driving erratically in a local restaurant parking lot. The person reporting provided police with a licence plate number of the subject vehicle. Police identified and spoke with the 44-year-old registered owner.
Dogs at large
A person reported three dogs at large from the backyard, through a hole in the fence, of a residence located in the area of 57 Street and 4900 Block. Police attended and located the dogs that were, at that point, inside the subject residence. Police noted a hole in the property fence. Camrose Community Peace Officer continued with follow-up investigation.
Check stop
Police conducted a traffic check stop and issued three warnings.
Theft
A representative of a local retail store reported a male subject committing theft. Police attended and located the 49-year-old male and issued a two-year trespass notice.
Stolen licence plate
A person reported the licence plate on their truck was stolen while the truck was parked near a downtown residence.
Vehicle hit
A person reported their vehicle was hit while parked in a local retail business parking lot. The matter is still under investigation.
Outstanding warrants
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for equipment violations and arrested the 40-year-old male operator of the vehicle on an outstanding warrant for assault and confinement. The male was housed in Camrose Police Service cells waiting judicial release.
Dog at large
A person reported being charged at by a dog in the area of 65 Street and 4300 Block. Police attended and located the subject dog which ran back into a residential yard, after which the gate was secured. Police spoke with the dog owner who was given a warning.
Possible theft
A person reported a male subject possibly stealing a bicycle and car, in the area of 50 Street and 4700 Block. The person reporting provided a description of the subject. Police conducted patrols of the area but the subject was not located. The is matter is still under investigation.
Vehicle violation
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for a vehicle violation The 28-year-old operator of the vehicle was issued violation tickets for operating a motor vehicle with a suspended driver’s licence, no vehicle registration and insurance. The vehicle was towed.
Prohibited weapons
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for equipment violations. The 46-year-old male operator of the vehicle was issued violation tickets for no insurance. The vehicle was towed. During the stop police seized a knife and imitation throwing star (weapons).
Impaired driving
A person reported a vehicle parked in a greenspace near the dog park. Police attended and located the subject vehicle noting a male sleeping in the driver’s seat. The 63-year-old male failed a Standard Field Sobriety test and was issued a 24-hour suspension from operating a motor vehicle and several violation tickets. The vehicle was towed.
Damage to property
A representative of a local retail business reported the window of the building had been shot with 4 BBs, causing approximately $1000 in damage. Police reviewed CCTV footage from a neighbouring business and obtained a vehicle description of a possible vehicle involved. This matter is still under investigation.
Speeding
Police conducted a traffic stop on a motorcycle for speeding and operator not wearing a helmet. The 34-year-old male operator of the motorcycle was issued violation tickets. The motorcycle was parked in a secure location until proper documentation provided.
Damage to vehicle
A person reported windows of their residence had been damaged, in the area of 36 Avenue and 5200 Block, with what appeared to be pellets, causing approximately $3000 in damage.

title: April 22-30
content:
Police attended two collisions, no injuries reported.
Police assisted EMS with five medical emergencies.
Police issued several traffic violations.
Notable Incidents
Causing disturbance
Police received several 911 calls regarding a male causing a disturbance by screaming and yelling in the downtown area. Police (including PACT team) attended, located and arrested a 34-year-old male.  The male was issued violation tickets for Causing a Disturbance by screaming and shouting in public.
Trespassing
A person reported a male trespassing on a property located in the area of 49 Street and 4700 Block. Police attended and observed a male (known to police) flee the area, evading police. Police later located and arrested the 45-year-old male subject who was charged with obstruction and breach of probation.
Attempt fraud
A person reported an unknown person forged a cheque (belonging to the person reporting), stolen earlier from their vehicle while it was parked at a local hotel. The cheque was stopped by the bank and no funds were lost.  This matter is still under investigation.
Driving complaint
A person reported a vehicle (description and licence plate number provided) driving unsafely in a school parking lot. Police identified the registered owner who indicated their son was driving the vehicle at the time of the complaint. Police spoke to and warned the son.
Public intoxication
Police observed two males involved in a dispute in a public place. Police arrested a 30-year-old male for public intoxication, he was transported to Camrose Police Service cells and released when sober.
Dogs at large
A person reported finding two dogs at large, taking them to their own residence awaiting police attendance. Police attended and took the dogs to a shelter.
Causing issues
A representative from a local retail business reported a male causing issues and making threatening comments. Police located the 66-year-old male subject and served him with a trespass notice.
Found scooter
An employee of a local retail store reported a mobility scooter left at the side of the business building. Police attended and seized the scooter for safe keeping.
Stolen bike
A person from a local church reported a bike left on church property (possibly stolen.) Police attended and notified the bike owner who retrieved the bike.
Speeding
A person reported a vehicle (description provided) speeding in the area of 60 Street and 55 Avenue. Police determined, contacted and warned the registered owner.
Noice concern
A person reported a car horn engaging for a long period. Police determined the registered owner who was issued a ticket for excessive noise.
Refuse to leave premises/outstanding warrant
A representative of a local motel reported an evicted tenant would not leave the property. Police attended and served the 27-year-old tenant and one other person with a trespass notice. One other 24-year-old female was arrested on an outstanding warrant.
Noise concern
A person reported excessive noise coming from a neighbouring garage at 3 a.m. Police attended and asked the 38-year-old male subject of the noise complaint to shut down the activity and noise.
Refuse to leave
A person requested police assistance in getting a known male to leave their premises. Police located the 25-year-old male subject who was too intoxicated to care for himself so was transported and housed in Camrose Police Service cells until sober.
On a separate occasion St. Mary’s Hospital security staff requested police assistance with removing a 56-year-old male refusing to leave the property. Police attended and assisted the male subject in leaving.
Damage to vehicle
A person reported the back window of their vehicle was smashed by a rock while the vehicle was parked at a local motel.
Stolen phone
A person (youth) reported their cellular phone was stolen from a locker at the Aquatic Centre by someone known to them. Police located and spoke with the youth suspect and their mother. The youth suspect admitted to stealing the phone and disposing of it in a garbage can. The youth subject was warned and the phone was located and returned to the owner.
Theft
A representative of a local retail business reported a 55-year-old female stole $100 worth of product from the business. The female was issued a trespass notice and the product was returned to the business.
Possible fraud
A person reported sending a large amount of money to an untraceable location after the caller was impersonating a relative.
Police warn the public on transferring money and gift cards to believed family and friends at the time. Always verify with other family or friends prior to sending any money to others.
Dog attack
A person reported their dog broke loose of their care while walking, chased and attacked a cat on private property. Camrose Community Peace Officer continues investigating the incident.
Excessive barking
A person reported two dogs in a residential backyard in the area of 34 Avenue and 6500 Block, barking excessively. Police attended and verified two dogs barking but no safety concerns. Police attempted to contact the owner of the dogs, unsuccessfully. This matter is still under investigation.
Breaching curfew
Police observed a 40-year-old male breaching his curfew. Police arrested and transported the male to Camrose Police Service cells.
No insurance
A person reported two vehicles revving engines in a local retail parking lot. Police located the subject vehicles and checks indicated one vehicle had mismatched licence plates and no insurance. The operator of the vehicle was issued violation tickets and the vehicle was towed.
Causing disturbance
A person reported a male subject causing a disturbance outside a local not-for-profit organization. Police located a 34-year-old male subject (known to police) nearby. The male was arrested and later released with a trespass notice. Shortly after the male was released, he was observed kicking the bay door of the Camrose Police Service (no damage.) The male was issued violation tickets and sent on his way.
Damage to property
A person reported the fuel tank of their vehicle had been drilled while parked in the area of 54 Avenue and 5100 Block.

title: March 1-7
content:
Police assisted EMS on three separate occasions with medical emergencies.
Police attended four collisions. No reported injuries.
Notable incidents
Stolen vehicle
A person reported their vehicle was stolen from their residence (while they were out of country.) This matter is still under investigation.
Outstanding warrants
Police conducted a traffic stop on a 38-year-old male known to have outstanding warrants. The male was arrested for $600 worth of fines.
Arrest without incident
A person reported an unwanted known male subject was in their residence and was not leaving. Police attended the residence, the reporting person had left the residence. Police contained the area, as the male subject had a past of violence and instability towards the police. The 40-year-old male subject was arrested without incident and held in custody.
Public intoxication
A person requested police assistance with removing a known female subject from their residence. Police attended and arrested the female subject for public intoxication. During incarceration the 34-year-old female subject became combative with police and needed to be restrained. The female was charged with obstruction and two counts of assault on a police officer.
Causing issues
A representative from a local retail business reported several youth causing issues. Police attended and located the youth, served them with trespass notices and contacted parents.
Damage to property and assault
A person reported a (know-to-them) 41-year-old male causing damage to their personal property and assaulting another male. The male was arrested by police and later released when sober with an indefinite trespass notice to the home
Expired registration
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for expired registration. The driver of the vehicle was unaware the vehicle was unregistered. The registered owner was contacted and will be issued violation tickets for no insurance and no registration.
Trespass
Police observed a known male subject contravening a trespass order. When police approached the male, he became confrontational. Police were able to calm the male and subsequently arrested him for obstruction and trespassing.
Equipment violation
Police conducted a traffic stop for an equipment violation. The operator of the vehicle was charged with no registration, misuse of licence plate and not carrying a bill of sale.
Breach of Release Order
Police observed a passenger in a motor vehicle that had a 24-hour curfew. The vehicle was stopped and the 29-year-old male was arrested and charged with two counts of breaching his court order.
Trespass
While on patrol police observed a male in a business contrary to his Trespass Notice. The 33-year-old male was approached and became aggressive towards police the male was arrested and charged with obstruction and trespassing.
Failed Field Sobriety Test
Police conducted a traffic stop for a vehicle equipment violation and observed  methamphetamine and a crack pipe with residue in the vehicle. The occupants of the vehicle were arrested. The 43-year-old driver of the vehicle failed a drug test and was charged accordingly. Tickets were also issued for no registration, no insurance and misuse of licence plates.
Impaired driver
Police received several reports regarding a possible impaired driver. Police located the subject vehicle, identified the operator of the vehicle and conducted tests for impaired driving. The 74-year-old male driver was charged with dangerous driving and other driving offences.
Impaired driving
Police conducted a traffic stop for equipment violations and observed the female operating the vehicle to be under the influence of alcohol. The 18-year-old female failed the roadside screening test and was charged with impaired driving.
Theft
A person reported theft of property valued at approximately $200.
Assault police officer
A person requested police assistance with a (known-to-them) 60-year-old male subject, who was drunk and being belligerent. Police attended and the male became uncooperative so was arrested Once in the police vehicle the male kicked the police vehicle and a police officer. The male was charged with assaulting a police officer.
Obstruction
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle. The driver of the vehicle and back seat passenger both lied about their identity. Police positively identified both individuals. The 19-year-old male passenger was arrested on a warrant and the 43-year-old female driver was charged with obstruction.

title: March 8-14
content:
Police attended four collisions. One with minor injuries reported.
Police assisted EMS with two medical emergencies.
Notable Incidents
Damage to vehicle
A person reported a rock had been thrown through the driver’s-side window of their vehicle while parked at 48 Street and 4900 Block.
Assault
Police responded to a report of a intoxicated,male subject who had assaulted the caller. The caller left the residence for their safety. Police attended and determined through a witness on scene that the subject had left the residence. The subject was observed by police in his vehicle speeding. The subject returned to the residence where he was arrested without incident. The 34-year-old male was charged with dangerous driving, unlawful confinement and assault.
Panhandling
Police responded to a complaint of a 26-year-old female subject panhandling outside a local retail business. Police attended and spoke with the subject and warned her about her behaviour. She agreed to leave the area.
Dog bite
A person reported being bitten (causing injury) by a dog in the area of 55 Street and 5300 Block. Police attended and spoke with the owner of the dog advising them to quarantine the dog while investigation ensues.
Camrose Community Peace Officer conducted a follow-up investigation on the dog bite incident. A statement was obtained and the matter is still under investigation.
Vandalism
A person reported vandalism at a local recreation centre. Police attended and reviewed CCTV footage which showed a male youth jumping on tables. Matter is still under investigation.
Theft
A representative of a local retail business reported a theft by a female and three male subjects. The person reporting indicated the subjects left in a white truck. A member of the RCMP, who was on scene at the time of the incident, attempted a stop on the subject vehicle, which fled westbound on Highway 13. Matter is still under investigation.
Curfew violation
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for an equipment violation. Two male subjects (44 and 47 years of age) were noted to be bound by an undertaking and curfew conditions. Both subjects were arrested.  Search of the vehicle, incident to arrest, revealed a small quantity of methamphetamine and drug paraphernalia.
Theft
A representative of a local retail business reported the possible theft of merchandise. The representative provided police with a description of the vehicle and licence plate number. Matter is still under investigation.
Unregistered vehicle
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle with expired registration. The operator of the vehicle was charged with no insurance and no registration. The vehicle was towed.
Assault
A 9-1-1 call reported an assault on an employee of a local restaurant by an unknown male subject. Police attended and noted the victim was in need of medical attention. EMS attended and transported the victim to St. Mary’s Hospital. Police reviewed CCTV footage and observed the assault. Patrols later located the 27-year-old male suspect who was arrested for assault and uttering threats and was transported to Camrose Police Service cells.
Outstanding warrants
Police conducted a traffic stop on a known 61-year-old male subject with warrants for $1,500 outstanding fines.
Impaired driving
Police conducted a traffic stop for a possible impaired driving. The 64-year-old male driver failed a Mandatory Alcohol Screening test, was issued an Immediate Roadside Sanction.
Arrest on warrant
Police conducted a traffic stop for an equipment violation. The 43-year-old male passenger was arrested on a warrant for $1,200 outstanding fines.

title: March 15-21
content:
Police attended 2 collisions, no injuries  reported.
Police assisted EMS with 1 medical emergency.
Notable Incidents
No insurance or registration
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for an equipment violation. The driver of the vehicle was charged with no insurance and no registration. The vehicle was towed.
Theft
A representative of a local retail business reported a possible theft of merchandise by two males with backpacks.
Theft of welding cables
A person reported the welding cables, valued at approximately $2,500, were cut off and stolen off their truck while parked in the area of 52 Avenue and 43 Street. This matter is still under investigation.
Theft from vehicle
A person reported their vehicle had been entered unlawfully while parked at 50 Avenue and 5200 Block, and sunglasses and a garage door opener were stolen.
Outstanding warrant
Police located a 33-year-old male wanted on outstanding warrants. The male subject was charged with two prior thefts from local retail businesses and was issued indefinite trespass notices.
Intoxicated male
A person reported they were having issues with a 20-year-old male subject who was intoxicated. Police attended the address subject to the complaint and arrested the male subject, to be released when sober.
Fraud
A person reported fraudulent charges on their credit card totaling approximately $100. Bank was notified and will be conducting investigation.
Speeding near school
A person reported a vehicle speeding in a school zone and the vehicle is currently parked near the school. Description of the vehicle and partial licence plate number was provided. Police spoke with the registered owner of the subject vehicle who indicated their vehicle was being driven by someone else at the time. Matter is still under investigation.
Theft arrest
Police located and arrested a 43-year-old male for a prior theft committed at a local retail business. The male was also issued trespass notices to some local businesses.
No registration/insurance
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for expired registration. The 46-year-old male driver was issued tickets for no registration and no insurance. The vehicle was towed.
Fraud
A person reported a fraud involving a travel company and an agreed upon repayment of $7,000. This matter is still under investigation.
Theft of parcel
A person reported the theft of a parcel at a residential address on Mount Pleasant Drive. This matter is still under investigation.
Waving a knife
A person reported a male subject outside of a local business waving a knife around. Police attended and located and arrested the 33-year-old male, who was known to police. During the arrest the male threw the knife on the ground. The subject was transported to Camrose Police Service cells.
Impaired driver
A person reported a vehicle driven suspiciously around the southside trailer park. Police attended and located the subject vehicle, noting the driver unconscious in the driver’s seat and the vehicle was still in drive. Police were able to gain access to the vehicle and wake the driver, who failed a roadside test for being under the influence of a drug. The 25-year-old female driver was arrested for impaired driving. A search of the vehicle and personal effects recovered two bottles of GHB (gamma-hydroxybutyrate), methamphetamine and a small amount of fentanyl. The vehicle was seized and the driver was issued a driving suspension and tickets for no insurance, no registration, no driver’s licence and equipment violation.
Theft
A representative of a local retail business reported two females committing theft. Police attended and reviewed CCTV footage, identifying both females. The two females (26 and 25 years of age), were located by police and issued trespass notices to the business. The merchandise was recovered.
Damage to vehicle
A person reported their vehicle was damaged while parked outside a local community hall. This matter is still under investigation.

title: March 22-31
content:
Police attended several collisions specifically on March 25 due to poor road conditions.
Police assisted EMS with 1 medical emergency.
Notable Incidents
Stolen truck
A person reported their truck stolen while parked over night at a local hotel.
Suspicious person
A person reported an unknown male in their garage in the area of 64 Street and 4800 Block. They provided a description and a name (given to them by the male subject). Police attended the male subject was gone upon police arrival.
Causing issues
A person reported a male subject causing issues at a local retail store/gas station. Police attended, located and arrested the 33-year-old male and transported him to Camrose Police Service cells.
Scam
A person reported being the victim of a fake not-for-profit scam, losing approximately $475.
Theft
A person reported the theft of copper wire worth approximately $5,000 from a local construction business.  The next day a person reported suspicious activity around the property of a local construction business. They provided police with a description of the vehicle involved. CCTV footage revealed the suspect vehicle. Further investigation revealed the theft of copper wire. Police located the subject vehicle parked at a local motel, noting copper wire inside the vehicle. Upon speaking with the registered owner of the vehicle they were able to ascertain and subsequently arrest two suspects in the earlier theft of copper wire. The two suspects aged 37-years-old and 43-years old were charged with Break and Enter with Intent to Commit Theft under $5,000, Possession of Stolen Property under $5,000 and Possession of Break In Instruments.
Intoxication in public place
Two people reported a male subject at a local restaurant inquiring about drugs and in possession of a bottle of alcohol. Police attended and arrested the 35-year-old male for public intoxication and transported him to Camrose Police Service cells.
Impaired driver
Police conducted a traffic stop on suspicion of impaired driving. Th 59-year-old female driver refused an Alcohol Screening Device test and was transported to Camrose Police Service cells where she was issued an Immediate Roadside Sanction, three violation tickets and housed until sober.
911 needing assistance
A female called 911 several times requesting assistance (then hanging up). Police quickly attended the subject address and arrested a 36-year-old male subject in breach of a no-contact order.
No registration, no insurance
Police conducted a traffic stop on an unregistered vehicle. The female operator of the vehicle was issued tickets for no registration and no insurance. The vehicle was towed.
Stolen wallet
A person reported their wallet was stolen out of a locker at the Aquatic Centre and that multiple unlawful charges were made on their credit card. The matter is still under investigation.
Erratic driving
A person reported a vehicle (description provided) driving erratically in a local parking lot. Police attended and conducted a traffic stop on the subject vehicle. Investigation is ongoing.
Outstanding warrants
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for no registration. The 23-year-old male driver had outstanding fines totaling $1,400. The male was issued tickets for no registration and no insurance.
Equipment violation
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for an equipment violation. The 19-year-old male operator of the vehicle failed a Mandatory Alcohol Screening Device test and was issued an Immediate Roadside Sanction. The vehicle was seized.
Nuisance dog
A person reported a nuisance dog barking. Police attended the subject residence of complaint and issued tickets to the homeowner.
Causing issues
Police received a report of an unknown, intoxicated male knocking on several doors in an apartment building in the area of Grand Drive. Police located the male subject (known to police) and issued him a trespass notice.
Possession prohibited weapon
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for an equipment violation. The 30-year-old female driver and 25-year-old female passenger were arrested. The driver was charged with possession of a prohibited weapon and illegal drugs and the passenger was charged with possession of a prohibited weapon.
Assault by youth
A person reported that a youth had been assaulted at a local recreation park by three other youth. The victim was taken to St. Mary’s Hospital and treated for minor injuries. After an investigation police located two youth and issued one with a violation ticket and the other with a trespass notice.
Outstanding warrants
Police arrested a 52-year-old male for outstanding fines totaling $3,000.
Misuse of licence plates
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for misuse of licence plates. The 60-year-old male operator of the vehicle was issued tickets for no insurance and other violations. The vehicle was towed.
Obstruction
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for equipment violations. The 59-year-old male operator of the vehicle refused a Mandatory Alcohol Screening test and was issued an Immediate Roadside Sanction. The male became difficult to deal with so was arrested for obstruction and transported to Camrose Police Service cells.

title: February 1-14
content:
Collision
Police responded to a one vehicle collision at 53 Street and 48 Avenue, involving a vehicle with a stop sign, (icy road conditions). City of Camrose Public Works department were contacted.
Refuse Mandatory Alcohol Screening test
Police observed a vehicle being operated on the wrong side of the roadway. Police conducted a vehicle stop. The operator of the vehicle refused a Mandatory Alcohol Screening test and received an Immediate Roadside sanction. The vehicle was seized and towed.
Drug arrest
On February 1, 2026 members of Camrose Police Service stopped a vehicle in the east end of Camrose for conducting an illegal U-turn.
The operator of the vehicle, identified as Crystal Luider, from Hanna, was issued a violation ticket.  Checks revealed that Luider had active charges for trafficking narcotics.
The front male passenger of the vehicle refused to identify himself, police however, noted the male had a large spider tattoo on his left hand.
Further checks by police to determine the identity of the male passenger resulted in a positive identification as Kenneth Dustin Powell from Drumheller. It was also determined Powell had a lengthy criminal record for trafficking narcotics, with an outstanding warrant for that activity, and was subsequently arrested.
A search of his person revealed a large quantity of drugs and drug paraphernalia.  The driver, Luider was arrested under the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act and a search of the vehicle revealed further drugs and drug paraphernalia.
In total, 29 grams of crack cocaine, 22 grams of methamphetamine, 18.5 grams of fentanyl, a scale, wrappers, cellphone and cash were seized. Powell was charged with three counts of Possession for the Purpose of Trafficking and Proceeds and Luider was charged with two counts of Possession for the Purpose of Trafficking. Both Luider and Powell were unable to meet court set bail and were remanded.
Collision
Police attended a collision on 68 Street and 4700 Block. The vehicle travelling south on 68 Street was struck by another vehicle. The operator of the other vehicle was charged.
Assault
A person reported being assaulted by a female subject. Police located and arrested and charged the female subject with assault
Collision
Police attended a collision at 65 Street and 4300 Block. The operator of a vehicle swerved to avoid a collision and struck a light pole causing significant damage to the vehicle and causing a power outage. Fortis attended. This matter is still under investigation.
Theft
An employee of a local restaurant reported a male had entered the restaurant and taken items. Police located and charged the male suspect.
Possible fraud
An employee of a local business reported a male subject suspected of using a stolen debit card. Police were able to locate and identify the suspect and the matter remains under investigation.
Erratic driving
Police responded to the are of Creekview and 50 Street after receiving reports of multiple vehicles driving erratically. Members located and stopped the vehicles. Three drivers were issued tickets for open alcohol, possession of illegal (untaxed) cigarettes and various vehicle equipment violations.
Stolen documents
A person reported their vehicle had been entered unlawfully while parked in a local retail parking lot. The vehicle’s registration and insurance documents were stolen.
Stolen tires and rims
A person reported two tires and rims were stolen from the box of their truck, at 48 Avenue and 54 Street.
Counterfeit bill
A representative from a local retail store reported receiving a counterfeit $50 bill.
Hit and run
Police received a report of a hit and run involving a vehicle colliding with a parked vehicle at 50 Avenue and 68 Street. Police located the subject vehicle. The operator of the vehicle failed a Mandatory Alcohol Screening test and was charged with an Immediate Roadside Sanction.
Damage to vehicle
Police responded to the 4300 block of 65 Street after receiving a report of a vehicle with a damaged windshield and driver’s-side window. Officers attended the scene and confirmed the damage. The investigation into this incident remains ongoing.
Theft
The representative of a local retail business reported a known male suspect had stolen a large quantity of product from the business. This matter is still under investigation.
Scam
A person reported being scammed by telephone out of $900 by someone claiming to be with a telephone provider.
Unregistered vehicle
Police conducted a traffic stop for an unregistered vehicle. The operator of the vehicle also had no insurance and was arrested on a warrant.
Outstanding warrant
Police observed and arrested a suspect with an outstanding warrant. The suspect was also charged with possession of illegal cigarettes (not marked for taxes.)
False identification
Staff at St. Mary’s Hospital reported a male in the emergency room providing inconsistent identification information. Police attended and located the individual, who then provided the officers with false identification. His true identity was later confirmed and he was found to be in possession of identity documents belonging to another person. The male was charged in relation to the offences.
Erratic driving
A representative of a local retail business reported a male subject driving erratically. Police located the subject vehicle. The operator of the vehicle was charged for not having insurance and a valid driver’ licence
Pointing firearm
Police responded to the downtown area for a report of a road rage incident. The investigation determined that a male driver had confronted another motorist and brandished a firearm. The suspect vehicle was later located, and police conducted a high-risk traffic stop. The driver and passenger were arrested, and a search of the vehicle recovered a firearm, later confirmed to be imitation, along with additional weapons. The driver was consequently charged in relation to the incident.
Unregistered and uninsured
Police conducted a stop on an unregistered vehicle. The operator and registered owner of the vehicle was charged with no registration and no insurance.
Causing concerns
A person reported a male subject causing concerns at a local bar, including punching another person causing injury. Bar staff detained the male subject until police arrived. The male subject was arrested and charged with assault causing bodily harm.

title: February 15-21
content:
Police attended several collisions, no reports of injury, and dealt with two separate incidents of operator of motor vehicle having no insurance and no vehicle registration.
Notable incidents
Fraud
A person reported their bank account was compromised resulting in the loss of nearly $3,000. The person reporting suspected an online fraud or hacking of their online banking information. A 57-year-ld male was arrested after he deposited a fraudulent cheque into his bank account. He was charged with Fraud Under $5,000 and released to appear in court.
Police remind residents to be very diligent about releasing any personal information online or over the telephone and never release information to unknown or unverified sources. Report any suspected fraud or scam activity to the police and/or online on the Government of Canada Report Cybercrime and Fraud website.
Assault
A person called 911 to report being assaulted by a known male. Police attended the area of the call and located a female victim and male suspect. The male suspect was arrested and charged with assault. The male also had a warrant for his arrest for outstanding fines.
Assault
A 911 caller reported being assaulted with a knife, by a youth known to the victim. Police attended and spoke with the caller who identified the suspect. Police located and arrested the youth who was charged with assault with a weapon.
Causing a disturbance
An employee of a local restaurant reported a male subject causing issues and refusing to leave the premises. Police attended and spoke to the male subject, who appeared agitated, but eventually left the premises.
Internal theft
The owner of a local restaurant reported an ex-employee had stolen $8,000 from the business. This matter is still under investigation.
Resist arrest/assault police officer
A person reported a possible fight occurring between a female and male at a neighboring residence. Police attended and located the male subject, determined to be intoxicated. The female had left prior to police arrival. The 50-year-old male subject was arrested for breaching alcohol conditions, during arrest he became uncooperative, resisting arrest. The male was charged with two counts of assault on a police officer, resisting arrest and uttering threats.
Impaired driver
Police conducted a traffic stop for equipment violations and noted the operator of the vehicle to be under the influence of alcohol. The operator failed the original roadside screening device test and was transported to Camrose Police Service where he provided samples of 290mg % and 270mg%. The 28-year-old male was charged and released on provincial driving sanctions.

title: May 1-7
content:
Police attended to three collisions. No reported injuries.
Police assisted EMS with four medical emergencies.
Police assisted Camrose Fire Department with two fire emergencies.
Notable Incidents
Smashed vehicle windows
A person reported their truck window was smashed and a generator and small bag stolen.
On a separate occurrence three other vehicles had windows damaged while parked at a local hotel. Another incident of a vehicle window damaged occurred while the vehicle was parked in the area of 45 Avenue and 6700 Block.
Theft
A loss prevention officer of a local retail business reported a female committing theft. The female left in a black truck. This matter is still under investigation.
Break and Enter mailboxes
Several people of a local apartment building reported two suspects (male and female) prying open mailboxes. This matter continues to be investigated.
Vandalism to private property
A person reported their driveway and garage door had paint poured on it.  Matter is still under investigation.
Encampment
Complaints were received about an unlawful encampment on CN property. Police attended and identified the person responsible for the encampment and gave them 24 hours to clean up the area.
Impaired
Police observed a 52-year-old male slumped over in the front driver’s seat of a running vehicle. The male failed an alcohol screening device test and was charged. The vehicle was towed from scene.
Public intoxication
Police observed and arrested a 20-year-old female for intoxication in a public place after reports of a female causing issues.
A person reported a male sleeping in the middle of the road near Spaceship Park. Police located the 33-year-old male who was intoxicated and subsequently transported him to Camrose Police Service.
Criminal harassment
After an investigation involving criminal harassment and distribution of indecent photographs police arrested a male. The matter is still under investigation.
Theft
Staff of a local retail business detained a female for theft of several products. The female was issued a trespass notice.
Assault
Several calls were received regarding a possible assault in the area of St. Mary’s Hospital. Police attended and located two male subjects. A 30-year-old male was arrested for assault and transported to Camrose Police Service cells.
Breach of Peace
A person reported a neighbour requesting their assistance. A 69-year-old male was refusing EMS entry to assist with a female inside the residence. The male was arrested to prevent breach of peace. The female was left in the custody of EMS.
Impaired driving/liquor in reach of driver
A person reported a male banging on their vehicle while parked in a local parking lot. Police attended and located the male subject who was issued an Immediate Roadside Sanction, trespass notice and violation tickets for liquor within the reach of driver.
Unlawful bank transfers
A person reported unlawful transfers of money made on their account totaling approximately $1800. The person indicated two possible suspects. This matter is still under investigation.
Theft
A representative of a local retail business reported a female subject stole merchandise from the store. Police attended, located and arrested the 38-year-old female for theft and during a search of her property recovered the stolen merchandise and a small amount of methamphetamine. The female was issued a trespass notice and the merchandise was returned to the business.
Fraud
A person reported an unknown subject opened a credit card account in his name. The card was cancelled at the time of the report and the bank is continuing to investigate.
Lost debit card
A person reported their debit card was lost and since two (unauthorized) transactions occurred. The card was cancelled through the bank and the matter remains under investigation.
Possible fraud
A person reported a possible bitcoin fraud.  The person was advised to contact Alberta Securities Commission to report the fraudulent activity.
Barking dog
A person reported a neighbour’s dog was barking excessively. Police attended and spoke with a male at the subject residence, who indicated he was caring for the dogs until the next day when they would return to their owner’s residence.
Causing issues
A representative of a local motel reported a male subject was evicted from the property but was refusing to leave. Police attended and told the 52-year-old male to leave the premises. The male left and continued to return on several occasions. The male was charged initially under the Trespass Act and eventually arrested for obstruction.
Theft
A Loss Prevention Officer from a local retail business reported having several individuals under arrest for theft. Police attended and issued the 41-year-old male and 42-year-old female with a trespass notice. The merchandise was returned to the business.
Fraud
A person reported a scam involving a false phone company and the purchase of a phone and tablet.
Theft
A representative of a local retail business reported having detained a male for theft. Police attended and issued the youth subject with a trespass notice and returned the merchandise to the business.
A local retail business reported four female subjects stole approximately $740 worth of merchandise. The merchandise was recovered and contained a wallet (possibly belonging to one of the suspects.) The four subjects left in a vehicle (description provided to police.) This matter is still under investigation.
Missing backpack
A person reported their friend’s backpack (which they had in their authorized possession) had been stolen while they were caring for the friend’s child at a playground. The bag contained two sets of vehicle fobs, a wallet containing identification cards and credit cards and some baby items.
Threat with weapon
A representative of a local restaurant reported a male (known and breaching a trespass notice) outside the restaurant causing issues and threatening customers. Police attended and located and arrested the male subject for possession of a dangerous weapon, uttering threats and breaching a Common Law Peace Bond. The subject was transported and housed at Camrose Police Service cells.

title: May 8-14
content:
Police attended two collisions. No injuries reported.
Police assisted EMS with four medical emergencies.
Notable Incidents
Bike patrol lends a hand
Camrose Police Service bike patrol escorted riders of the St. Patrick Catholic School annual Bike Bus event.
Counterfeit bill
A local bank reported an unknown person passed a $20 counterfeit bill which was included in a deposit. Later in the week a representative of a local convenience store attended Camrose Police Service and turned in a $20 counterfeit bill that was passed by an unknown customer.
Breach of Peace
Several people reported a female and male arguing outside of a local business, and that the female assaulted the male.  Police located the female and arrested her to prevent a further breach of peace. The female was charged with assault and released from custody.
Theft
A Loss Prevention Officer of a local retail business reported having detained a female subject for theft. Police attended and issued the female with a trespass notice. The merchandise was returned to the business.
Impaired driving
A representative of a local business reported a male appeared to be passed out in a running vehicle. Police attended and observed the male passed out in the driver’s seat of the running vehicle. The male provided police with a breath sample test which he failed. The male was issued with an Immediate Roadside Sanction and the vehicle was towed.
Assault police officer
A person reported loud music coming from a neighbouring apartment in the area of 53 Street and 4900 Block. Police attended and heard the music outside the building. The male subject of the complaint was identified and asked by police to turn down the music. The male became uncooperative and physical with police and resisted arrest. Police deployed a CEW (Conducted Energy Weapon) and were able to effectively arrest the male. The male was charged with Causing a Disturbance, Assault a Police Officer and Resisting Arrest
Unlawful encampment
A person reported an unlawful encampment in the area of 54 Avenue and 63 Street. Police attended and located three individuals. All occupants were provided support options but declined. They were directed to clean up the area by morning.
Warrants
Police conducted a traffic stop for a suspected impaired driver. The 19-year-old male operator of the vehicle was given an alcohol screening test and was issued an Immediate Roadside Sanction and violation tickets. The vehicle was towed.
Youth assault
A complaint was received of two youths fighting in a business in the downtown area. One of the youths was known to police. After an initial investigation it was determined one of the youths was responsible for the fight, including information that he brandished a knife during the interaction. The youth was issued a trespass notice. Investigation concerning the weapon continues.
No vehicle insurance/registration
Police conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle for an unregistered vehicle. The vehicle also had no insurance and the male operator did not produce a valid driver’s licence. The male was issued violation tickets and the vehicle was towed.
Damaged property
A person reported the back door of their residence appeared to be tampered with and was damaged (approximately $100-$200.) Police attended and canvased the neighbourhood for CCTV footage and possible witnesses. This matter is still under investigation.
Scam
A person reported a video chat with a person alleging to be from known not-for-profit organization indicating she won a large sum of money. When the person reporting refused to cooperate with the caller, the caller became belligerent.
Police remind all residents of he numerous scams that are circulating and advise to never give out any personal information to someone they do not know or can not verify. When in doubt Hang Up.
Outstanding warrant
Police conducted a traffic stop and arrested a 56-year-old male with outstanding warrants. The male paid the $1250 in outstanding fines and was released.
Black bear
A person reported a black bear in the Victoria Park area heading towards the golf course. Police attended and located the bear which was running on the train tracks west.  Camrose and District Fish and Wildlife were notified.
